Nigeria says two nationals killed in South Africa amid rise of anti-migrant attacks
clusters.summary: Nigeria's government said one of its nationals was reportedly killed by police officers "using gruesome interrogation techniques".

clusters.headline: Super Typhoon Bavi makes landfall on US Pacific islands with huge wind gusts
clusters.summary: The storm, with winds of nearly 290km/h (180mph) and gusts of 350km/h, is lashing the island of Rota.

clusters.headline: Thousands welcome home Cape Verde footballers after stunning World Cup run
clusters.summary: Tens of thousands of fans formed an ocean of blue outside the airport in capital city, Praia.

clusters.headline: Philippine Vice President Duterte’s impeachment trial begins: What we know
clusters.summary: Duterte faces charges of mishandling government funds and making threats during rift with President Ferdinand Marcos Jr.

clusters.headline: The African fishermen who blame Chinese trawlers for their woes
clusters.summary: Fishing crews in Sierra Leone say large Chinese ships are illegally hoovering up stocks.

clusters.headline: From 'a beautiful night' to 'world's greatest hangover', guests react to Taylor and Travis's wedding
clusters.summary: Some of the famous guests have been posting on social media about the big day in New York.

clusters.headline: Liberia: Absence of Suspects Charged in Absentia in U.S.$19m Drug Bust Could Derail Probe
clusters.summary: [FrontPageAfrica] Monrovia -- Nearly a month after authorities seized US$19 million worth of cocaine at Roberts International Airport, five people have been formally charged and named as principal suspects in a case that is once again drawing global scrutiny of Liberia's much-publicized war on drugs

clusters.headline: 'I buried my parents one day after the other' - Ebola mourners learn how to grieve safely
clusters.summary: A cemetery at the epicentre of the outbreak is rapidly filling up, however large traditional funerals have been scrapped.

clusters.headline: 'I ate ketchup and cheese,' says Venezuelan girl trapped under quake rubble for 32 hours
clusters.summary: Fabiana was trapped in the rubble of a 10-storey residential building after two earthquakes rocked Venezuela in June.

clusters.headline: 'My stomach is eating itself': Biohacker Bryan Johnson reveals he has autoimmune disease
clusters.summary: The tech entrepreneur, who is known for spending about US$2 million a year on longevity treatments, said he was diagnosed with autoimmune gastritis after months of medical tests.
